channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Puntos clave de la crisis en la Casa Blanca por los archivos Epstein
item.description: Dos reporteros del Times detallan los enfrentamientos en las más altas esferas del gobierno estadounidense para manejar el escándalo del delincuente sexual y sus conexiones con el presidente Trump.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan
item.category: Regime Change: Inside the Imperial Presidency of Donald Trump (Book)

media:content.media:description: Manifestantes frente a la Casa Blanca el pasado mes de julio.

item.title: ¿Cuánta desnudez permite Instagram?
item.description: La única constante en las normas sobre la publicación de desnudos en la plataforma de redes sociales parece ser su inconsistencia a la hora de aplicarlas.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Bernstein

media:content.media:credit: Ilustración de The New York Times; Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Los creadores de contenido que han sido suspendidos se preguntan por las normas de la comunidad de Meta.

item.title: Delaney Hall Is Named After Their Family. They Want It to Close.
item.description: Members of the Delaney family say that if the center continues to be used as an Immigration and Customs Enforcement facility, its name needs to be changed.
item.dc:creator: Claire Fahy

media:content.media:description: Geraldine Owen Delaney opened Delaney Hall as a rehabilitation center and halfway house with a mission that is misaligned with its use as an ICE detention facility.

item.title: A Great Run for the Knicks Means a Great Run for T-Shirt Bootleggers
item.description: The demand for Knicks merchandise is through the roof, with official retailers and designer boutiques selling out. For $20, the guys on the sidewalks around Madison Square Garden have you covered.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Maslin Nir

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Starting from a wholesaler in the Bronx named Frenchie, the bootleg Knicks T-shirt makes its way into the hands of a New Yorker for $20.

item.title: For Knicks Fans at Rucker Park, Hope, Doubt and a Desire for Revenge
item.description: Some fans at the world-famous basketball court in Harlem are swept up in the excitement of the N.B.A. finals with the Knicks one win from a championship.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Southall

media:content.media:credit: Reece Williams for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Janell Moore, 30, said she rooted for the Knicks while she was growing up in Germany.

item.title: Así fue el pánico en la Casa Blanca por los archivos Epstein
item.description: Los principales asesores del presidente se reunieron en una serie de sesiones en la Sala de Situación para intentar contener un escándalo que involucraba a Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan
item.category: Regime Change: Inside the Imperial Presidency of Donald Trump (Book)

item.title: It’s Been 100 Days. Do You Know Where Rep. Tom Kean Is?
item.description: The New Jersey representative, Thomas Kean Jr., voted in the House and spoke at a committee hearing on March 5. He hasn’t been spotted since.
item.dc:creator: Tracey Tully

media:content.media:description: Representative Thomas Kean Jr. has been absent from the public eye for more than three months.

item.title: Has There Ever Been a Crazier Sports Moment for New York? Actually, Yes.
item.description: In the spring of 1994, the World Cup arrived, the Knicks were great and so were the Rangers. And in the middle of it all, an infamous White Bronco chase.
item.dc:creator: David Waldstein

media:content.media:description: Just as the World Cup arrived in New York, the 1994 Knicks made a run the N.B.A. finals, hunting their first championship in decades. Sound familiar?

item.title: Can’t Afford Summer Camp? These Playground Workers Can Help.
item.description: City parks officials will put about 70 seasonal playground associates in New York City parks this summer to run free games and activities for any young person who shows up.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Hu

media:content.media:credit: José A. Alvarado Jr. for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Playground associates work with thousands of children at New York City parks over the course of the summer.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for June 13, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: El regreso de Conor McGregor: sustancias prohibidas y un médico famoso
item.description: La sorprendente transformación física de McGregor ha sido objeto de especulaciones entre los aficionados de la UFC, los luchadores y comentaristas que han afirmado que eludió las reglas antidopaje para usar sustancias que mejoran el rendimiento mientras se recuperaba.
item.dc:creator: Michael S. Schmidt

media:content.media:description: Conor McGregor preparándose para pelear contra Dustin Poirier en Las Vegas en 2021. McGregor sufrió una fractura grave en la pierna durante el combate.

item.title: L.A. Kicks Off the World Cup With a Party
item.description: Fans, many draped in flags and colorful costumes, arrived at SoFi Stadium hours before the United States began its campaign against Paraguay.
item.dc:creator: Tim Arango and Jesus Jiménez

item.title: 6 formas de hacer ejercicio en el agua
item.description: Ya empieza la temporada de playa y piscinas. ¿Por qué no hacer un poco de ejercicio mientras disfrutas del sol?
item.dc:creator: Erik Vance

media:content.media:credit: From left: Zack Wittman, Bryan Bennett and Logan Cyrus for The New York Times

item.title: Quote of the Day: In Gray Area of Not Peace and Not War
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Saturday, June 13, 2026.

item.title: Should Switzerland Cap Its Population at 10 Million? Voters Will Decide.
item.description: One of the world’s richest countries is about to hold a referendum on a measure that would curb migration and most likely the economy. It is being sold in warm tones.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley

media:content.media:description: Campaign posters ahead of the population cap vote. President Trump’s face is on a no poster, with the slogan, “Now, of all times, a break with Europe?” The yes slogan shown is “Protect Switzerland.”

item.title: A Family Ravaged by Land Mines in Myanmar
item.description: Bu Ri lost a leg to a mine in Myanmar decades ago. More recently, six other members of his family have suffered a similar fate or worse, in a sign of the devastation wreaked by civil war.
item.dc:creator: Richard C. Paddock

item.title: How a 10 Million Population Cap Would Change Switzerland
item.description: On Sunday, Switzerland will vote on whether to cap its population. The implications for Swiss society and its economy could be huge.
item.dc:creator: Adina Renner and Jim Tankersley

item.title: New Yorkers Are Going on the Road With the Knicks for Game 5
item.description: One resale site said that more than 48 percent of its tickets to the game in San Antonio had been sold to people from New York and New Jersey.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Maag

media:content.media:description: Knicks fans at Frost Bank Center in San Antonio for Game 2 of the N.B.A. finals last week.

item.title: One Dead and Several Injured in Tent Collapse Outside Virginia Church
item.description: Severe weather conditions caused the collapse during an outdoor service and concert Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Madaleine Rubin

media:content.media:credit: Veasey Conway for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The EastLake Community Church in Moneta, Va., in 2021.

item.title: Anthropic Blocks Foreigners From Using Mythos and Fable AI
item.description: The company said on Friday night that the federal government had ordered limits on its Mythos and Fable 5 A.I. systems, citing national security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Dustin Volz

media:content.media:description: Anthropic’s Mythos A.I. model has raised worries that it could be used to attack computer networks.

item.title: Festival Flier
item.description: Ryan McCarty’s themeless puzzle is tough but fair.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:description: Good luck steering your way through this twisty solve!

item.title: In Rare Move, D.S.A Rebukes Mamdani Over Police Plans
item.description: The New York City chapter of the Democratic Socialists of America criticized the mayor over his support for increasing the Police Department’s head count.
item.dc:creator: Maia Coleman

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The New York City chapter of the Democratic Socialists of America said Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s support of a plan to increase the Police Department’s head count “runs counter to the values of the socialist and working-class movement that elected him.”

item.title: Judge Dismisses Republican Group’s Case Against the University of Florida
item.description: A campus group the university banned after accusations of antisemitic behavior failed to prove it had its First Amendment rights violated, the court ruled.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Saul

media:content.media:credit: Charlotte Kesl for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The University of Florida College Republicans came under scrutiny in March.

item.title: Still Need to Book a Summer Trip? Here Are Our Suggestions on Where to Go
item.description: Once again, readers wrote in with their questions and the travel experts answered.
item.dc:creator: Deborah Dunn

media:content.media:description: A farm on Île d’Orléans, an island in the St. Lawrence River that’s a short drive from downtown Quebec City.

item.title: Musk Is the World’s First Trillionaire. Who Was the First Billionaire?
item.description: John D. Rockefeller, the Gilded Age oil baron, became America’s first billionaire in 1916. Like Elon Musk’s ascension, the milestone made headlines.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald

media:content.media:description: John D. Rockefeller Sr., in Lakewood, N.J., in 1933. Rockefeller, the Gilded Age oil baron, became a billionaire in 1916.

item.title: ‘Almost There, Almost There, Almost There’: The Hopes and the Heartbreak of the '90s Knicks
item.description: 28 years ago, the team’s nemesis Reggie Miller sunk a shot that dashed their playoff dreams. Here’s what Spike Lee and others sitting courtside remember.
item.dc:creator: Katherine Cusumano

item.title: Gene Shalit, Film Critic Bristling With Hair and Puns, Dies at 100
item.description: One of the nation’s most recognizable characters, he delivered his wacky commentary for more than 40 years on the “Today” show.
item.dc:creator: Robert D. McFadden

media:content.media:description: Gene Shalit in 1980. With his handlebar mustache, bushy hair, black horn-rimmed glasses and extravagant bow ties, he was one of the nation’s most recognizable characters.

item.title: Judge Blocks National Parks From Removing ‘Negative’ Signs and Depictions of Slavery
item.description: The ruling accused the Trump administration of engaging in censorship by taking down materials at parks across the country.
item.dc:creator: Maxine Joselow

media:content.media:credit: Hannah Beier for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: An outdoor exhibit on slavery at Independence National Historical Park in Philadelphia, seen last year, was taken down in January.

item.title: What It Means for Elon Musk to Be Worth $1 Trillion
item.description: We used physical dollar bills to see just how far $1 trillion could go.
item.dc:creator: Lily Boyce

item.title: Afghans Hold Rare Public Protests Against Taliban Rules
item.description: The United Nations said it was “deeply concerned” about the arrests of dozens of women, and reported that two people were killed in protests organized to support them.
item.dc:creator: Yaqoob Akbary and Zane Irwin
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:description: In Herat, Afghanistan, on Monday.

item.title: Trump Administration Says It Will Restart Asylum and Immigration Processing
item.description: The response came after a federal judge rebuked officials for failing to immediately comply with the order he issued last week.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Ngo and Zach Montague

media:content.media:credit: Madison Swart for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: An immigration court in New York in May. More than a million applications for asylum and immigration had ground to a halt under Trump administration policies.

item.title: U.S. Says Iran Cease-Fire Deal ‘Very Close’
item.description: A senior administration official said the two sides were “not quite at the finish line yet.”
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Swan and David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Avocado Salad With Herbs and Capers
item.description: This salad is both simple to make and highly luscious. It gets a velvety richness from the avocados and is enlivened by a splash of red wine vinegar and a handful of briny capers sprinkled on top.

media:content.media:credit: Andrew Scrivani for The New York Times

item.title: Tahini and Chocolate Chunk Cake
item.description: In this cake, developed by the NYT Cooking contributor Samantha Seneviratne, tahini flavors a one-bowl yellow cake, giving it a pillowy texture and nutty flavor. Swirled with chocolate, it’s reminiscent of your favorite halvah candy.

media:content.media:credit: Julia Gartland for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Spencer Richards.

item.title: Influential Artist David Hockney Dies at Age 88
item.description: The artist David Hockney, 88, died at his home in London on Thursday. Mr. Hockney’s brightly colored paintings depicting California defied the mid-20th century’s dominant abstract schools and drew attention to figurative art.
item.dc:creator: Christina Kelso

media:content.media:credit: Fairchild Archive/Penske Media, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Mr. Hockney working on “Parade,” a 1981 triple bill at the Metropolitan Opera and one of his renowned stage designs.

item.title: La Iglesia española espera que la visita del papa León atraiga a los jóvenes al catolicismo
item.description: La visita de León XIV a España, que terminó el viernes, ha despertado entre los líderes católicos la esperanza de un renacimiento espiritual.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz

media:content.media:description: El papa León XIV llega a la plaza de Lima de Madrid el sábado 6 de junio para una vigilia de oración con los jóvenes en el primer día de una visita de siete días a la península ibérica y las islas Canarias.

item.title: NASA Leader Responds to Criticism Over All-Male Artemis III Crew
item.description: NASA’s missions these days rarely feature all-male crews. Jared Isaacman, its administrator, said women play prominent roles throughout the space agency.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth Chang

media:content.media:description: From left, the crew of the Artemis III: Randy Bresnik, Luca Parmitano, Frank Rubio and Andre Douglas.

item.title: How Betters Use Arbitrage to Make Free Money on Kalshi and Polymarket
item.description: How bettors are turning statistical glitches into guaranteed profits.
item.dc:creator: Evan Gorelick and Katherine Chui

item.title: Elon Musk Becomes the World’s First Trillionaire
item.description: Also, officials say a U.S.-Iran cease-fire appears within reach. Here’s the latest at the end of Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

item.title: Justice Dept. Clears Way for Paramount-Warner Bros. Merger
item.description: The $111 billion deal would unite two major movie studios and put CNN under the same roof as CBS News.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hirsch, David McCabe and Benjamin Mullin

media:content.media:credit: Mark Abramson for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A merger of Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery would include the Warner Bros. movie studio, above, in Burbank, Calif.

item.title: Tornadoes Tear Through Midwest States
item.description: Tornadoes were reported in Illinois, Indiana and Wisconsin on Thursday, bringing powerful winds, heavy rain and hail.
item.dc:creator: David Cheung

item.title: Gavin Newsom and Hunter Biden Would Like Your Attention, Please
item.description: The two men chatted on Mr. Newsom’s podcast about Democrats, Joe Biden, Graham Platner, phone addiction and other topics.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein

media:content.media:credit: Max Whittaker for The New York Times, Valerie Plesch for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gov. Gavin Newsom of California hosted Hunter Biden on his podcast this week.

item.title: Reto de verano: saca tu teléfono del cuarto para dormir mejor
item.description: Solo te tomará unos minutos
item.dc:creator: Jancee Dunn

item.title: 審判はあなた！
item.description: メジャーリーグの審判たちが、史上初となるボール・ストライク判定のビデオレビューに直面しています。あなたなら、正しく判定できるでしょうか？
item.dc:creator: Michael Beswetherick and Sean Catangui

item.title: Sé un umpire de las Grandes Ligas
item.description: Por primera vez, los umpires de las Grandes Ligas se enfrentan a la revisión por video de las bolas y los strikes. ¿Qué tal lo harías tú?
item.dc:creator: Michael Beswetherick and Sean Catangui

item.title: Trump’s Haphazard Midterm Leadership Puts Republicans in a Bind
item.description: The president and his party have had a rough start to June, after a spring full of MAGA primary victories and redistricting gains.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck

item.title: Western Australia Is Battling a Mouse Plague
item.description: For months, mice have been found in tea kettles, crunched by car tires and even appeared in people’s beds. In one town, the end might be in sight.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung

item.title: Plague of Mice Hits Western Australia
item.description: Parts of Western Australia have been battling a mouse plague for months, as swarms of the rodent have taken over homes, businesses and roads.
item.dc:creator: Jiwoong Hong

item.title: Two Dan Sullivans? Alaska Republicans Suggest Kicking One Off the Ballot in Senate Race
item.description: The lieutenant governor and the top elections official, both Republicans, are investigating whether the challenger coordinated with a Democrat to confuse voters.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk and Kellen Browning

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times, Karen Dillman, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Republicans argue that the Senate campaign by Dan J. Sullivan, right, was planned by Democrats to confuse voters and hurt Senator Dan S. Sullivan, left, a second-term lawmaker.

item.title: SpaceX’s Unlikely Journey From Far-Out Idea to $2 Trillion Juggernaut
item.description: Elon Musk said he had initially given SpaceX less than a 10 percent chance of succeeding. His rocket company has come a long way.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac, Kenneth Chang and Kirsten Grind

media:content.media:credit: Andres Kudacki for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A SpaceX ad on the Nasdaq MarketSite tower in Times Square. The company, which Elon Musk founded in 2002, began trading on the stock market on Friday.
